name: Malta
excerpt: With a civilization that dates back thousands of years, Malta boasts some of the oldest megalithic sites in the world.

quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Maltese (descendants of ancient Carthaginians and Phoenicians with strong elements of Italian and other Mediterranean stock)
quick_facts.climate: Mediterranean; mild, rainy winters; hot, dry summers
quick_facts.terrain: mostly low, rocky, flat to dissected plains; many coastal cliffs
quick_facts.major_exports: refined petroleum, integrated circuits, packaged medicine, ships, postage stamps/documents (2023)

summaries.geography_summary: the country is an archipelago, with only the three largest islands (Malta, Ghawdex or Gozo, and Kemmuna or Comino) inhabited; numerous bays provide good harbors
summaries.history_summary: With a civilization that dates back thousands of years, Malta boasts some of the oldest megalithic sites in the world. Situated in the center of the Mediterranean, Malta’s islands have long served as a strategic military asset, with the islands at various times falling under the control of the Phoenicians, Carthaginians, Greeks, Romans, Byzantines, Moors, Normans, Sicilians, Spanish, Knights of St. John, and French. Most recently a British colony (since 1814), Malta gained its independence in 1964 and declared itself a republic 10 years later. While under British rule, the island staunchly supported the UK through both world wars. Since the mid-1980s, the island has transformed itself into a freight transshipment point, a financial center, and a tourist destination, as its key industries moved toward more service-oriented activities. Malta became an EU member in 2004 and joined the eurozone in 2008.
summaries.government_summary: parliamentary republic. Independence: 21 September 1964 (from the UK)
summaries.economy_summary: high-income, EU-member European economy; diversified portfolio; euro user; dependent on food and energy imports; strong tourism, trade, and manufacturing sectors; high North African immigration; large welfare system; educated workforce

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json

prayer_and_mission.prayer_intro: Malta's Catholic identity is nearly universal and constitutionally established, leaving very little room historically for evangelical witness. Pray for the clear gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone to reach a nation where Catholic tradition runs deep.
prayer_points: Pray for laborers to plant independent, Bible-believing local churches across a nation with very little evangelical presence.
Pray for Maltese practicing inherited Catholic tradition to encounter genuine, personal faith in Christ.
prayer_and_mission.mission_emphasis: With over 90 percent of the population Roman Catholic by official identity, Malta represents a significant gospel need with very little existing evangelical presence. Ongoing needs include training national pastors and planting autonomous local churches practicing believer's baptism by immersion.
